---
category: productivity
title: Doc Coauthoring
name: doc-coauthoring
description: "Use when writing documentation, technical specs, decision documents, user guides, or API documentation through iterative collaborative drafting."
---

## Description

Guide users through structured workflow for co-authoring documentation. Helps transfer context efficiently, refine content through iteration, and verify documentation works for readers.

## When to Use

- Writing documentation for projects
- Creating proposals or technical specs
- Drafting decision documents
- Building user guides or tutorials
- Creating API documentation
- Collaborative documentation projects

## When NOT to Use

- Quick inline comments or notes
- Code documentation (use code-docs)
- Real-time chat or messaging
- Non-documentation content

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Transfer Context

- Discuss documentation goals
- Identify target audience
- Gather existing materials
- Outline structure

### Phase 2: Refine Content

- Draft sections iteratively
- Get feedback on clarity
- Revise based on feedback
- Improve organization

### Phase 3: Verify & Test

- Have readers review
- Test instructions if applicable
- Verify completeness
- Check for accuracy

### Phase 4: Finalize

- Polish language and formatting
- Add examples and diagrams
- Prepare for publication
- Set up maintenance plan

## Tools & References

- **Related Skills**: writing-clearly-and-concisely, project-docs
- **Formats**: Markdown, RST, AsciiDoc
- **Collaboration**: Version control, review process

## Best Practices

- Start with clear goals
- Involve target audience early
- Iterate frequently
- Keep documentation in sync with code
- Use consistent formatting
- Include examples and diagrams
- Maintain documentation regularly
